channel.title: ainews — Karl's daily AI news
channel.description: Daily AI news, deduped + tagged for hotloop's workflows

item.title: Sanders and AOC introduce AI Data Center Moratorium Act
item.description: Senators Bernie Sanders and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez introduced the AI Data Center Moratorium Act, calling for a pause on major AI data center construction and frontier AI development to allow time for safety evaluation. Marks the first significant US legislative attempt to slow frontier AI expansion.

item.title: LeCun: LLMs fail at physical world understanding — JEPA/world models are the path forward
item.description: Yann LeCun argues in a multi-tweet thread that LLMs perform worse than a cat at anticipating physical-world consequences, making them unsuitable for robotics. Predicts JEPA-style world models will supersede token-based generative architectures for next-generation embodied AI.

item.title: Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0
item.description: xAI launches Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0, combining real-time speech interaction with fast-reasoning capability. Adds a competitive low-latency voice AI option for developers building conversational agents and voice-first applications.

item.title: Federated Learning Without the Refactoring Overhead Using NVIDIA FLARE
item.description: NVIDIA FLARE enables federated learning without requiring developers to refactor existing ML training code, using adapter layers to privacy-preserve distributed training. Targets healthcare, finance, and regulated sectors where training data cannot leave local environments.

item.title: Sources: China recently told tech companies, including ByteDance and Moonshot AI, to reject US capital without state approval, after Meta's Manus acquisition (Bloomberg)
item.description: Bloomberg : Sources: China recently told tech companies, including ByteDance and Moonshot AI, to reject US capital without state approval, after Meta's Manus acquisition   —  Chinese regulators plan to restrict technology firms including some of the country's highest-profile AI pioneers …

item.title: Alibaba says its Qwen AI models will be integrated into BYD, Volkswagen, and other cars, letting users buy food and tickets via voice commands on select models (Evelyn Cheng/CNBC)
item.description: Evelyn Cheng / CNBC : Alibaba says its Qwen AI models will be integrated into BYD, Volkswagen, and other cars, letting users buy food and tickets via voice commands on select models   —  BEIJING — Chinese tech giant Alibaba said Friday that its Qwen artificial intelligence model will be…

item.title: OpenAI Codex ships auto-review: guardian agent cuts human approval overhead
item.description: Greg Brockman announces auto-review is live in OpenAI Codex: a guardian agent evaluates whether proposed actions are safe before execution, reducing required human approvals to only the most critical cases. Directly addresses a key friction point in production agentic deployments.

item.title: Tracxn: global edtech funding fell from $16.7B in 2021 to $2.6B in 2025, while the number of startups launched dropped from 10,491 in 2021 to just 645 in 2025 (Ananya Bhattacharya/Rest of World)
item.description: Ananya Bhattacharya / Rest of World : Tracxn: global edtech funding fell from $16.7B in 2021 to $2.6B in 2025, while the number of startups launched dropped from 10,491 in 2021 to just 645 in 2025   —  Venture capital is moving away from K-12 edtech worldwide as investors prioritize AI tools and…

item.title: DeepSeek V4 Pro costs $1.74/1M input tokens and $3.48/1M output tokens, while V4 Flash costs $0.14/1M and $0.28/1M; both models are the cheapest in their class (Simon Willison/Simon Willison's Weblog)
item.description: Simon Willison / Simon Willison's Weblog : DeepSeek V4 Pro costs $1.74/1M input tokens and $3.48/1M output tokens, while V4 Flash costs $0.14/1M and $0.28/1M; both models are the cheapest in their class   —  Chinese AI lab DeepSeek's last model release was V3.2 (and V3.2 Speciale) last…

item.title: Sources: Cursor hit $2.7B in annualized sales in March, up ~14x from a year ago, and reported a ~$900M loss in its last fiscal year on $770M in total revenue (The Information)
item.description: The Information : Sources: Cursor hit $2.7B in annualized sales in March, up ~14x from a year ago, and reported a ~$900M loss in its last fiscal year on $770M in total revenue   —  Weeks before Cursor agreed to a $60 billion potential takeover from SpaceX, the hot AI coding startup set out to…

item.title: China's top market regulator says it is launching a six-month crackdown on the country's online ad sector, targeting malpractices including the misuse of AI (Ben Jiang/South China Morning Post)
item.description: Ben Jiang / South China Morning Post : China's top market regulator says it is launching a six-month crackdown on the country's online ad sector, targeting malpractices including the misuse of AI   —  Market watchdog flags AI abuse and traffic-driven tactics as it launches first campaign to…

item.title: DeepSeek V4 - almost on the frontier, a fraction of the price
item.description: Chinese AI lab DeepSeek's last model release was V3.2 (and V3.2 Speciale) last December . They just dropped the first of their hotly anticipated V4 series in the shape of two preview models, DeepSeek-V4-Pro and DeepSeek-V4-Flash . Both models are 1 million token context Mixture of Experts. Pro is 1.6T total…

item.title: DeepSeek V4 Pro has 1.6T total parameters, its largest model by that metric, and V4 Flash has 284B parameters; both models have a context window of 1M tokens (South China Morning Post)
item.description: South China Morning Post : DeepSeek V4 Pro has 1.6T total parameters, its largest model by that metric, and V4 Flash has 284B parameters; both models have a context window of 1M tokens   —  The company says its cost-efficient new V4 model is competitive with top closed-source models from OpenAI…

item.title: Huawei says its Ascend supernode based on the Ascend 950 AI chips will fully support DeepSeek V4, as DeepSeek launches a preview of its V4 model (Reuters)
item.description: Reuters : Huawei says its Ascend supernode based on the Ascend 950 AI chips will fully support DeepSeek V4, as DeepSeek launches a preview of its V4 model   —  Huawei Technologies said on Friday its Ascend supernode based on Ascend 950 AI chips would fully support Deepseek's V4 versions …

item.title: [AINews] GPT 5.5 and OpenAI Codex Superapp
item.description: Spud lives!

item.title: Millisecond Converter
item.description: Tool: Millisecond Converter LLM reports prompt durations in milliseconds and I got fed up of having to think about how to convert those to seconds and minutes. Tags: tools

item.title: It's a big one
item.description: This week's edition of my email newsletter (aka content from this blog delivered to your inbox) features 4 pelicans riding bicycles, 1 possum on an e-scooter, up to 5 raccoons with ham radios hiding in crowds, 5 blog posts, 8 links, 3 quotes and a new chapter of my Agentic Engineering Patterns guide. Tags: newsletter

item.title: russellromney/honker
item.description: russellromney/honker "Postgres NOTIFY/LISTEN semantics" for SQLite, implemented as a Rust SQLite extension and various language bindings to help make use of it. The design of this looks very solid. It lets you write Python code for queues that looks like this: import honker db = honker . open ( "app.db" ) emails…

item.title: Singapore emerges as neutral ground for AI firms navigating Sino-US rivalry
item.description: Reuters reports Singapore is transforming from an East-West gateway into a neutral ground for the AI sector, with Chinese startups relocating or opening offices there to sidestep US and Chinese regulation. Adds a third hub to the global AI map alongside the Bay Area and Shenzhen.

item.title: DeepSeek-V4: a million-token context that agents can actually use
item.description: HuggingFace's technical analysis of DeepSeek V4 highlights the practical utility of its million-token context window for long-horizon agentic workflows. Covers both V4-Pro (1.6T parameters, MoE) and V4-Flash, positioning DeepSeek V4 as a cost-effective near-frontier model that competes on pricing with top closed-source offerings.

item.title: Extract PDF text in your browser with LiteParse for the web
item.description: Simon Willison ships a browser-based PDF text extraction tool built with Claude Code. Uses spatial parsing without AI plus optional OCR; demonstrates how AI-assisted coding compresses side-project development to hours.

item.title: AI coding firm Cognition in early talks to raise funds at $25B valuation
item.description: Cognition AI, maker of the Devin coding agent, is in early talks to raise hundreds of millions at a $25B valuation, up from $10.2B in September 2025. The round signals continued appetite for agentic-coding startups despite the crowded field.

item.title: Winning a Kaggle Competition with Generative AI-Assisted Coding
item.description: Three LLM agents generated 600,000+ lines of code and ran 850 experiments to win a Kaggle playground competition. Demonstrates how agentic coding plus GPU acceleration compresses the ML development cycle from weeks to days.

item.title: A pelican for GPT-5.5 via the Codex backdoor API
item.description: Simon Willison builds an llm-openai-via-codex plugin to access GPT-5.5 through the Codex subscriber API before the official API launch. Finds it highly capable but notes the model's default output is less polished than extended-reasoning mode.

item.title: AIE Europe Debrief + Agent Labs Thesis (Latent Space × Unsupervised Learning)
item.description: Swyx and Jacob Effron discuss AIE Europe takeaways and the emerging 'agent lab' playbook: domain-specific model training, harness engineering, memory systems, and world models as intelligence primitives. State of AI coding wars — multi-billion ARR products emerging.

item.title: llm-openai-via-codex 0.1a0
item.description: Release: llm-openai-via-codex 0.1a0 Hijacks your Codex CLI credentials to make API calls with LLM, as described in my post about GPT-5.5 . Tags: openai , llm , codex-cli

item.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Powers Codex on NVIDIA Infrastructure
item.description: NVIDIA deploys GPT-5.5-powered Codex company-wide on GB200 NVL72 systems with 10,000+ employees using it. Debugging cycles reduced from days to hours — enterprise proof-of-concept for frontier model plus specialised inference hardware.

item.title: GPT-5.5 pricing: $5/M input, $30/M output — double GPT-5.4
item.description: GPT-5.5 is priced at $5 per million input tokens and $30 per million output tokens, double GPT-5.4; GPT-5.5 Pro costs $30 input and $180 output. VentureBeat notes the model narrowly beats Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview on Terminal Bench 2.0.

item.title: GPT-5.5
item.description: Article URL: https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-5/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47879092 Points: 1387 # Comments: 910

item.title: MeshCore development team splits over trademark dispute and AI-generated code
item.description: The MeshCore project has split following disputes over the project's trademark and contributions of AI-generated code. Highlights governance friction as open-source projects grapple with AI-tool usage norms.
